summary: Matched the common side-tilt attack code by moving the `ftCo_AttackS3` routines out of `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` into a new C translation unit and header. The PR added `ftCo_AttackS3_8008B658`, the side-tilt motion selection helper, and the standard Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks, then rewired existing IASA call sites from the old `ft_8008B658` symbol to the new namespaced symbol. It also added minimal data declarations needed for matching, including `ftData_80085FD4` and several `ftCommonData` floats used as stick-angle thresholds.
what_changed: Created `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_AttackS3.c` with matched C for the removed assembly range `8008B658` through `8008B97C`.

Added `ftCo_AttackS3_8008B658`, which checks A-button input, forward stick threshold, vertical stick magnitude, held item state, and item kind before choosing between light throw, item attack, item throw, or side-tilt motion selection.

Added a static local helper `decideAngle` corresponding to address `8008B788`, selecting among `ftCo_MS_AttackS3Hi`, `ftCo_MS_AttackS3HiS`, `ftCo_MS_AttackS3S`, `ftCo_MS_AttackS3LwS`, and `ftCo_MS_AttackS3Lw` based on `ftCommon_8007D964(fp)`, common-data thresholds, and whether fighter motion data exists.

Implemented `ftCo_AttackS3_Anim`, `ftCo_AttackS3_IASA`, `ftCo_AttackS3_Phys`, and `ftCo_AttackS3_Coll` in C, forwarding to existing helpers such as `ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining`, `ft_8008A2BC`, `ftCo_Wait_IASA`, `ft_80084F3C`, and `ft_80084104`.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_AttackS3.h` with declarations for the new side-tilt functions.
Updated `obj_files.mk` so `ftCo_AttackS3.c.o` is built before the remaining `ft_08A4.s.o` assembly object.
Removed the old `ft_8008B658` declaration from `src/melee/ft/ft_0877.h` and updated C and assembly call sites to use `ftCo_AttackS3_8008B658`.

Changed remaining assembly references in `ft_08A4.s` from `ft_8008B658` to `ftCo_AttackS3_8008B658`, including IASA paths for low tilt, smash, guard off, ottotto, walk, turn, landing, squat, squat wait, squat reverse, and appeal.

Promoted a remaining assembly local label `.L_8008BA38` to global `lbl_8008BA38`, apparently so the partially decompiled assembly file can still branch/link cleanly after extracting the preceding C routines.
Added a declaration for `ftData_80085FD4(Fighter* fp, FtMotionId msid)` returning a small struct with an `x8` field, used to test whether a motion animation pointer/data entry exists.
Refined `ftCommonData` around offsets `0x98` to `0xA8`, replacing padding with named floats `x98_shieldHealthInit`, `x9C`, `xA0`, `xA4`, and `xA8` needed by the side-tilt angle thresholds.
smart_moves: Kept the still-unknown entry function partially address-named as `ftCo_AttackS3_8008B658`, which improves subsystem locality without pretending the exact semantic name is known.
Used a static helper `decideAngle` for the former local label at `8008B788`, isolating the side-tilt variant selection logic from the input/item gatekeeper.
Preserved short-circuit IASA behavior by updating all callers to the new symbol instead of changing surrounding control flow.

Guarded side-tilt variant selection with `ftData_80085FD4(...)->x8 != NULL`, matching the assembly pattern that checks whether the fighter has data for a specific motion before selecting that variant.
Replaced hard-coded common-data padding with specific float fields only where the matched function needed them, avoiding a broad speculative struct expansion.
Left an explicit `@todo` on whether the custom `ABS` macro is standing in for a fake `fabs_inline`, signaling a matching workaround rather than presenting it as final style.
decomp_lessons: When extracting one routine from a large assembly file, update every remaining assembly and C call site to the new symbol so the mixed C/asm boundary stays stable.
For common-action IASA helpers, preserve boolean-return short-circuit order exactly; many states call the same input check as part of long transition chains.
Motion selection code often needs both analog-stick thresholds from `p_ftCommonData` and per-fighter motion-data availability checks before choosing a variant.
Do not over-name uncertain helper functions: a subsystem prefix plus address suffix, as in `ftCo_AttackS3_8008B658`, is preferable to a misleading semantic name.
Small anonymous or placeholder struct declarations can unblock a match when only one field, here `x8` from `ftData_80085FD4`, is needed by the current decompilation.
Mixed C/assembly extraction may require promoting a local assembly label to a global `lbl_...` symbol if other code still needs a linkable branch target.
Common fighter action files should get their own `ftCo_ActionName.c` and `ftCo_ActionName.h` and be added to `obj_files.mk` near related ftCommon objects.
naming_conventions: `ftCo_AttackS3.c` and `ftCo_AttackS3.h` follow the common fighter action file naming pattern.
Standard callbacks use the action suffix plus lifecycle names: `ftCo_AttackS3_Anim`, `ftCo_AttackS3_IASA`, `ftCo_AttackS3_Phys`, and `ftCo_AttackS3_Coll`.
Unresolved public helper names can combine the action namespace and original address, e.g. `ftCo_AttackS3_8008B658`.
Former local assembly labels that must become link-visible use `lbl_` plus address, e.g. `lbl_8008BA38`.
Header guard used the address-style convention `GALE01_08B8D4`.
Common-data fields were named by offset when semantics were not fully known, e.g. `x9C`, `xA0`, `xA4`, and `xA8`, while an existing known semantic field remained `x98_shieldHealthInit`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed about 253 lines of hand-written assembly for the matched side-tilt routines and replaced them with 106 lines of C plus a small header.
Used a macro `ABS(x) ((x) < 0 ? -(x) : (x))` with a `@todo` noting uncertainty about whether an inline fabs pattern was needed for exact codegen.
Kept `NULL` and `1.0f` constants generated from C by deleting the corresponding `.sdata2` labels `.L_804D8468` and `.L_804D846C` from assembly.

Matched branchy item logic by preserving the two-stage `it_8026B30C` behavior: a zero result or held LR triggers `ft_800957F4(..., ftCo_MS_LightThrowF)`, while item kinds `2` and `3` call `ftCo_Attack_800CCF58(gobj, 1)` and `ftCo_Attack_800CDD14(gobj)` respectively.
Matched side-tilt startup by clearing `fp->x2218_b0`, calling `Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, msid, Ft_MF_None, NULL, 0, 1, 0)`, then `ftAnim_8006EBA4(gobj)`.
Preserved assembly file continuity by leaving later `ft_08A4.s` routines in place and only renaming their calls to the extracted C symbol.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
Because there was no human discussion in the slice, there is no direct evidence of reviewer rationale beyond the merged diff itself.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further decompile `ftCo_AttackS3_8008B658` to a semantic helper name.
Find later PRs that replace the anonymous `ftData_80085FD4` return struct with a named motion-data type.
Find later PRs that resolve the `ABS`/`fabs_inline` todo in `ftCo_AttackS3.c`.
Search for later struct cleanup around `ftCommonData` offsets `0x9C`, `0xA0`, `0xA4`, and `0xA8`.
Search for PRs that remove or decompile the remaining `lbl_8008BA38` region from `ft_08A4.s`.
